calendar.matters:
PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Todd Moeller for Petitioner, Monique Young

Mr. Moeller spoke of German law requiring funds for minors to be held in a custodial account until the minor reaches age 18, asked if the court would need to see redacted bank statements, and about his dealing with ancillary in Massachusetts which took effort. The Court stated it is satisfied with his explanation.

The Second amended Petition for approval of Status Report of Administrator is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Wu. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-29-2019 Continued To Oct-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Continued To Sep-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Todd Moeller for Monique Young.

The Court noted that a supplemental accounting was not really necessary and that it included a property in Massachusetts that is beyond the Court's jurisdiction. Mr. Moeller stated that he included the property because there was a question as to whether this estate received assets from Massachusetts and that the language in the order from Massachusetts distributed directly to the beneficiaries. The Court stated that it is inclined to put the issue of the property aside and that if the parties need an order regarding the property they can get it from Massachusetts. Mr. Moeller also stated that one of the beneficiaries (Sabine) died, that distribution was made to the personal representative (her Husband), that the German Court issued a certificate of inheritance, that 50% goes to the spouse and 50% goes to the children, and that they distributed a check to the estate of Sabine Sperling. In addition, Mr. Moeller stated that he did not get an apostille, that there is a seal and that because it is a public document it is perceived to be true and correct on its own. The Court stated that it would rather see an apostille, that Germany is a party to the Hague Convention, raised the issue of the requirement of a process to protect the minors' interests, stated that it needs some assurance that the minors interests are protected/proof of a blocked account particularly with respect to Maya, that the supplemental accounting raised the issue of attorney's fees paid for work on the accounting and that it was already compensated in the fees to the administrator. Mr. Moeller stated that he did it intentionally, that this estate wasn't ordinary, that the decedent left no paper trail, that there were complicated issues with the property, that there were six estate income taxes and that he filed an accounting because they took a large reserve. The Court stated that it is inclined to order reimbursement. If Mr. Moeller is requesting compensation for those fees he will have to amend his pleading to include a request for extraordinary fees. Mr. Moeller explained that the request for reimbursement of $435.00 was $200.00 for the filing of April 16th and $200.00 for the filing of May 30th, that the attorney service charged a $20.00 fee and a $15.00 fee, and that he filed a status report because he did not want to miss the ball. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to September 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide more information as to the protection of the minor's interests, and for filing of an amendment with respect to attorney's fees and more information regarding the filing fees. The supplemental accounting is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
